item.title: One Model to Translate Them All: Universal Any-to-Any Translation for Heterogeneous Collaborative Perception
item.content:encoded: By sharing intermediate features, collaborative perception extends each agent's sensing beyond standalone limits, but real-world feature modality heterogeneity remains a key barrier to effective fusion. Most existing methods, including direct adaption and protocol-based transformation, typically rely on training adapters for newly emerging feature modalities and often require additional retraining or fine-tuning. Such repeated training is costly and is often infeasible across manufacturers due to model and data privacy constraints, limiting real-world scalability. To address this issue, we propose UniTrans, a universal any-to-any feature modality translation model that instantiates translators on the fly for arbitrary modalities. UniTrans pretrains a bank of translator expert parameters and learns their combination coefficients as a function of source-to-target modality mapping. The mapping is measured in a modality-intrinsic latent space, where an intrinsic encoder extracts modality-specific yet scene-invariant codes from single-frame intermediate features, enabling UniTrans to instantiate translators in a zero-shot manner. Experiments on OPV2V-H and DAIR-V2X demonstrate that UniTrans consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both simulated and real-world settings, enabling efficient any-to-any translation through a universal model. The code is available at https://github.com/CheeryLeeyy/UniTrans.

item.title: Prior Knowledge or Search? A Study of LLM Agents in Hardware-Aware Code Optimization
item.content:encoded: LLM discovery and optimization systems are increasingly applied across domains, implementing a common propose-evaluate-revise loop. Such optimization or discovery progresses via context conditioning on received feedback from an environment. However, as modern LLM agents are increasingly complex in their structure, it is difficult to evaluate which components contribute the most, and when and how this exploration may fail. We answer these questions through three controlled experiments. Our findings: (1) In pure black-box optimization, LLMs act as greedy optimizers. (2) In zero-shot kernel generation, providing explicit input-size information has no measurable effect, models converge to the same kernel parameters regardless of size or temperature, as though the size instruction were invisible. Moreover, when tasked to perform kernel optimization for uncommon kernel sizes, performance sharply degrades regardless of the language used. (3) In feedback-loop kernel optimization, CUDA improves monotonically under iterative feedback, while TVM IR actively degrades, which demonstrates that kernel optimization degrades when models operate with low-density language. Our results conclude that LLMs in code optimization tasks highly depend on pretrained priors rather than provided feedback or agentic structure.

item.title: Dual-Prompt CLIP with Hybrid Visual Encoders for Occluded Person Re-Identification
item.content:encoded: Occluded person re-identification focuses on matching partially visible pedestrians across multiple camera views. However, occlusions disrupt body-region cues, thereby complicating cross-view matching. Most person ReID methods built on pretrained vision-language models only focus on enhancing prompt-based feature learning while ignoring the semantic information of occluders. Based on the success of CLIP-ReID, we propose a novel Dual Prompt Learning ReID (DPL-ReID) model for occluded person ReID. It incorporates a Dual Prompt Learning (Dual-PL) strategy, which can utilize textual cues to capture complete pedestrian semantics and keep robustness against occlusion, and a Real-World Occlusion Augmentation (RWOA) method that realistically simulates occlusion scenarios encountered in real word to enrich occluded samples. In addition, we also design a Weighted Gated Feature Fusion (WGFF) method, which in corporates LSNet to capture global information and act as a feature-gating mechanism. This mechanism can effectively guide the CLIP visual encoder toward generating more comprehensive feature representations. Extensive experiments on several benchmark occluded ReID datasets show that our proposed DPL-ReID achieves the state-of-the art performance. The occlusion instance library are available at https://github.com/stone-qiao/DPL-ReID.

item.title: SceneCode: Executable World Programs for Editable Indoor Scenes with Articulated Objects
item.content:encoded: Indoor scene synthesis underpins embodied AI, robotic manipulation, and simulation-based policy evaluation, where a useful scene must specify not only what the environment looks like, but also how its objects are structured. Existing pipelines, however, typically represent generated content as static meshes and inherit articulation only from curated asset libraries, which limits object-level controllability and prevents new interactable assets from being produced on demand. We address this gap by formulating physically interactable indoor scene synthesis as programmatic world generation, and present SceneCode, a framework that compiles a natural language prompt into an executable, code-driven indoor world rather than a collection of opaque meshes. A room-level agentic backbone first turns the prompt into a structured house layout and emits per-object AssetRequests through a planner--designer--critic loop. Each request is then routed to one of five code-generation strategies and converted into a synthesized part-wise Blender Python programs that are validated through an execution-guided repair-and-refine loop. The resulting programs are compiled into simulation-ready assets, and exported as SDF for physics simulation. A persistent scene-state registry links object requests, executable programs, rendered geometry, and simulation assets, turning scene assembly into a traceable and locally editable world-building process. We evaluate SceneCode across scene-level synthesis, object-level asset quality, human judgment, and downstream robot interaction. Results show that executable world programs improve prompt-faithful indoor scene generation and produce assets with cleaner mesh structure, and simulator-loadable articulation metadata. Project page: https://scene-code.github.io/.

item.title: From Seeing to Thinking: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning Improves Post-Training of Vision-Language Models
item.content:encoded: Recent advances in vision-language models (VLMs) emphasize long chain-of-thought reasoning; yet, we find that their performance on visual tasks is primarily limited by a lack of visual perception as opposed to reasoning itself. In this work, we systematically study the interplay between perception and reasoning in VLM post-training by decomposing their capabilities into three separate training stages: visual perception, visual reasoning, and textual reasoning, incorporating specialized training data. We demonstrate that visual perception (a) requires targeted optimization with specialized data; (b) serves as a fundamental scaffold that should be solidified through staged training before refining visual reasoning; and (c) is more effectively learned via RL than caption-based SFT. Our experiments across multiple VLMs demonstrate that staged training consistently improves both visual perception and reasoning performance over merged training. Notably, models trained with our approach achieve 1.5% higher reasoning accuracy with 20.8% shorter reasoning traces, suggesting that superior perception reduces the need for excessive reasoning. Furthermore, we show that this capability-based staging represents a new curriculum dimension orthogonal to traditional difficulty-based curricula, and combining both yields further additive gains. Our staged-training models achieve superior performance among open-weight VLMs, establishing advanced results on several visual math and perception (e.g., +5.2% on WeMath and +3.7% on RealWorldQA) tasks compared with the base counterpart.

item.title: Tabular foundation models for robust calibration of near-infrared chemical sensing data
item.content:encoded: Near-infrared spectroscopy is increasingly used as a rapid, non-destructive chemical sensing technology for the analysis of food, pharmaceutical, biological, and environmental samples. However, the practical deployment of NIR sensors still depends on calibration models able to handle high-dimensional, collinear spectra, limited sample sizes, preprocessing dependence, spectral outliers, and extrapolation beyond the calibration domain. Here, we evaluate whether tabular foundation models can provide a new calibration strategy for NIR chemical sensing. We benchmark TabPFN on 66 NIR datasets covering 54 regression and 12 classification tasks, and compare direct inference on raw spectra with preprocessing-optimized inference against PLS/PLS-DA, Ridge, Catboost, and one-dimensional convolutional neural networks. The study uses a unified validation framework in which preprocessing and model selection are performed exclusively on calibration data before external test evaluation. In regression, preprocessing-optimized TabPFN achieves the best overall average rank and significantly outperforms PLS, CatBoost, TabPFN on raw spectra, and CNN-1D, while remaining statistically comparable to Ridge. In classification, TabPFN applied directly to raw spectra provides the best average rank, with performance close to the optimized variant. Robustness analyses show that TabPFN provides strong average predictive performance but that its advantage decreases on spectral outliers and extrapolated samples, where classical chemometric models remain competitive. These results suggest that tabular foundation models can complement established chemometric workflows for NIR chemical sensing, especially in small- to medium-sized calibration settings, while highlighting the need for spectroscopy-specific priors and uncertainty-aware deployment strategies.

item.title: QLIF-CAST: Quantum Leaky-Integrate-and-Fire for Time-Series Weather Forecasting
item.content:encoded: Accurate and efficient time-series forecasting remains a challenging problem for both classical and quantum neural architectures, particularly in multivariate environmental settings. This work adapts the Quantum Leaky Integrate-and-Fire (QLIF) spiking neural network for time-series regression tasks, specifically short-term multivariate weather forecasting. We extend QLIF beyond classification and demonstrate its applicability to continuous-valued prediction problems. The QLIF-CAST model encodes neuron excitation states as single-qubit quantum superpositions, driven by Rx rotation gates and T1 relaxation decay, and is embedded within a hybrid quantum-classical recurrent architecture. We conduct two distinct evaluations. First, a controlled comparison against a parameter-matched classical LIF baseline on a multivariate weather dataset shows that QLIF-CAST achieves 15.4% lower MSE and 4.4% lower MAE, demonstrating that quantum neuronal dynamics reduce prediction error over classical equivalents. Second, a cross-domain comparative analysis with state-of-the-art quantum LSTM (QLSTM) and quantum neural network (QNN) models on air quality and wind speed benchmarks reveals that QLIF-CAST converges in up to 94% less training time, occupying a distinct position in the speed-error trade-off space. Hardware verification on IBM Marrakesh (156-qubit QPU) confirms reliable circuit execution with only 1.2% average deviation from simulation.

item.title: Rethinking Token Reduction for Diffusion Models via Output-Similarity-Awareness
item.content:encoded: Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) achieve superior image generation quality but suffer from quadratic computational complexity relative to token count. While various token reduction (TR) methods have been proposed to mitigate this cost, they overlook the primary objective of generative models: minimizing recovery error, which requires reflecting output token similarity. They rely solely on input token similarity inherited from reduction-only ViT paradigms, leading to a fundamental misalignment with this objective. To bridge this gap, we propose DiTo, a novel TR paradigm that shifts the focus toward output-centric token reduction. Based on the observation that output token similarity is consistently preserved across adjacent timesteps, DiTo utilizes prior-step similarities as an effective proxy to establish token correspondences at a Matching timestep, which are then reused across multiple subsequent Reduction timesteps. To optimize this interleaved scheduling, we propose Pair Match Ratio (PMR)-guided Interval Scheduling to determine the optimal matching frequency. Furthermore, to mitigate localized approximation errors and resulting blocking artifacts caused by repeated reuse, we propose Frequency-aware Token Matching by incorporating a selection-frequency penalty. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DiTo consistently outperforms existing TR methods with 1.6-3.9 dB higher PSNR at comparable speedups, achieving a superior Pareto frontier.

item.title: What Makes a Representation Good for Single-Cell Perturbation Prediction?
item.content:encoded: Single-cell perturbation modeling is fundamental for understanding and predicting cellular responses to genetic perturbations. However, existing approaches, from causal representation learning to foundation models, often struggle with an overlooked challenge: gene expression is dominated by perturbation-invariant information, while perturbation-specific signals are intrinsically sparse. As a result, learned representations either entangle invariant and perturbation-specific information, leading to spurious and non-generalizable predictors, or suppress perturbation-specific signals altogether, rendering them ineffective for prediction. To address this, we propose PerturbedVAE, a general framework designed to resolve this signal imbalance. The framework explicitly separates perturbation-specific information from dominant invariant structure and recovers causal representations to effectively utilize such information for prediction. We further provide an identifiability analysis that characterizes the conditions under which sparse perturbation effects can be reliably recovered, thereby clarifying how the framework can be concretely specified under such conditions. Empirically, PerturbedVAE achieves state-of-the-art performance on a widely used benchmark across multiple evaluation settings, yielding significant gains on out-of-distribution combinatorial predictions and uncovering interpretable perturbation-response programs.

item.title: When Accuracy Is Not Enough: Uncertainty Collapse between Noisy Label Learning and Out-of-Distribution Detection
item.content:encoded: Learning with noisy labels (LNL) is typically benchmarked by closed-set classification accuracy, yet deployment often requires classifiers to reject out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs. We present a learner-agnostic ACC-OOD benchmark that freezes LNL checkpoints and evaluates them with standardized near-/far-OOD routing and post-hoc scores across synthetic and real label noise. The benchmark reveals a recurring failure mode: high closed-set accuracy does not ensure OOD reliability, because low-confidence, misclassified in-distribution samples can overlap the score and feature regions occupied by OOD inputs under noisy training. We term this pathology uncertainty collapse. This structural overlap can make high-accuracy LNL methods lose separability at the ID-error/OOD interface under standard OOD scores. As an intervention, we study Virtual Margin Regularization (VMR), a lightweight repair probe demonstrated mainly with PSSCL that synthesizes boundary virtual outliers on trusted ID batches and widens the energy margin. VMR partially reduces the collapse-induced far-OOD failure without replacing the host objective or sacrificing closed-set accuracy in the tested settings. These results support LNL benchmarks that co-report closed-set generalization, open-world reliability, and structural overlap diagnostics.

item.title: Probabilistic Multivariate Time Series Forecasting with Diffusion Copulas
item.content:encoded: Accurately assessing financial risk requires capturing both individual asset volatility and the complex, asymmetric dependence structures that emerge during extreme market events. While modern diffusion-based models have advanced multivariate forecasting, they often suffer from a "normality bias" when trained end-to-end, sacrificing marginal calibration for joint coherence and consistently underestimating tail risk. To address this, we propose a Diffusion-Copula framework that explicitly decouples the learning of marginal distributions from their dependence structure. We employ deep Mixture Density Networks to capture heavy-tailed asset dynamics, followed by a Classification-Diffusion Copula to model the joint dependence. Applied to cryptocurrency markets, our approach demonstrates superior performance over state-of-the-art baselines in forecasting systemic extremes of both marginal and joint events. Crucially, we demonstrate that while baseline models classify simultaneous market crashes as statistically impossible "Black Swans" (high surprise), our framework identifies them as "Expected Crashes" (low surprise), successfully preserving the correlation structure necessary for robust risk management during contagion events.

item.title: Internalizing Tool Knowledge in Small Language Models via QLoRA Fine-Tuning
item.content:encoded: Large language models are increasingly used as planning components in agentic systems, but current tool-use pipelines often require full tool schemas to be included in every prompt, creating substantial token overhead and limiting the practicality of smaller models. This paper investigates whether tool-use knowledge can be internalized into small language models through parameter-efficient fine-tuning, enabling structured planning without explicit tool descriptions at inference time. Using AssetOpsBench as the primary benchmark, we fine-tune Gemma 4 E4B and Qwen3-4B with 8-bit QLoRA on approximately 1,700 tool-use examples spanning tool knowledge, question-to-plan mappings, and execution-style traces. We evaluate the resulting models under description-free inference, where the prompt omits the tool catalog entirely. The fine-tuned models outperform an informed unfine-tuned baseline that receives full tool descriptions, reducing input length by 82.6\% while improving structural and LLM-judge planning scores. In the best Gemma run, the model achieves an AT-F1 of 0.65 and an overall judge score of 3.88, compared with 0.47 and 2.88 for the informed baseline. Qwen3-4B achieves a strong overall judge score of 3.78 while using 62\% less memory and running 2.5$\times$ faster than Gemma, though it also exhibits greater catastrophic forgetting on general multiple-choice benchmarks. Additional ablations show that LoRA rank controls a quality--retention trade-off, with $r=32$ maximizing planning quality and smaller ranks preserving more general knowledge. These results suggest that, for fixed tool catalogs, QLoRA fine-tuning can shift tool knowledge from prompt context into model weights, substantially reducing inference overhead while maintaining or improving tool-planning quality.

item.title: CPC-VAR:Continual Personalized and Compositional Generation in Visual Autoregressive Models
item.content:encoded: Visual autoregressive (VAR) models have recently emerged as an efficient paradigm for text-to-image generation. Despite their strong generative capability, existing VAR-based personalization methods remain limited to static settings, failing to accommodate evolving user demands. In particular, sequential concept learning leads to severe catastrophic forgetting, while multi-concept synthesis often suffers from feature entanglement and attribute inconsistency. In this work, we present the first systematic study of continual personalized generation in VAR models. We identify two key challenges: (i) preserving previously learned concepts during sequential customization, and (ii) composing multiple personalized concepts in a controllable manner. To address these issues, we propose a unified framework with two core components. For continual single-concept learning, we introduce Gradient-based Concept Neuron Selection (GCNS), which identifies concept-relevant neurons and constrains only conflicting parameters across tasks, effectively mitigating forgetting without additional model expansion. For multi-concept synthesis, we propose a context-aware composition strategy that performs multi-branch feature modeling and localized cross-attention fusion guided by spatial conditions, enabling precise and disentangled concept composition. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method significantly improves performance in long-sequence continual personalization while achieving superior results in multi-concept image synthesis compared to existing baselines. These findings highlight the potential of VAR models for scalable and controllable personalized generation.

item.title: Automated ICD Classification of Psychiatric Diagnoses: From Classical NLP to Large Language Models
item.content:encoded: Mental health has become a global priority, leading to a massive administrative burden in the coding of clinical diagnoses. This study proposes the automation of psychiatric diagnostic analysis by mapping free-text descriptions to the International Classification of Diseases (ICD) using Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Machine Learning (ML) techniques. Utilizing a specialized dataset of 145,513 Spanish psychiatric descriptions, various text representation paradigms were evaluated, ranging from classical frequency-based models (BoW, TF-IDF) to state-of-the-art Large Language Models (LLMs) such as e5\_large, BioLORD, and Llama-3-8B. Results indicate that transformer-based embeddings consistently outperform traditional methods by capturing implicit semantic cues and nuanced medical terminology. The e5\_large model, through end-to-end fine-tuning, achieved the highest performance with a $F1_{micro}$ score of 0.866. This research demonstrates that adapting LLMs to specific clinical nomenclature is essential for overcoming the challenges of ``long-tail'' label distributions and the inherent ambiguity of psychiatric discourse.

item.title: Findings of the Counter Turing Test: AI-Generated Text Detection
item.content:encoded: The rapid proliferation of AI-generated text has introduced significant challenges in maintaining the integrity of digital content. Advanced generative models such as GPT-4, Claude 3.5, and Llama can produce highly coherent and human-like text, making it increasingly difficult to differentiate between human-written and AI-generated content. While these models have transformative applications, their misuse has raised concerns about misinformation, biased narratives, and security threats. This paper provides a comprehensive analysis of state-of-the-art AI-generated text detection techniques and evaluates their effectiveness through the Counter Turing Test (CT2) shared tasks. Task A (Binary Classification) required participants to distinguish between human-written and AI-generated text, while Task B (Model Attribution) focused on identifying the specific language model responsible for generating a given text. The results demonstrated high performance in binary classification, with the top system achieving an F1 score of 1.0000, but significantly lower scores in model attribution, where the best system achieved 0.9531, highlighting the increased complexity of this task. The top-performing teams leveraged fine-tuned transformer models, ensemble learning, and hybrid detection approaches, with DeBERTa-based and BART-based methods demonstrating strong results. However, the lower scores in Task B underscore the challenges of distinguishing outputs from different LLMs, necessitating further research into adversarial robustness, feature extraction, and cross-domain generalization.

item.title: 基于功耗感知的LLM推理运行时系统PALS
item.content:encoded:
PALS提升LLM推理能效并减少QoS违规。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为PALS的功耗感知运行时系统，用于优化大型语言模型（LLM）推理。该系统将GPU功耗限制作为可调控资源，与软件参数如批处理大小联合优化，以提高能效并满足吞吐量目标。PALS在不改变模型或API的情况下集成到vLLM框架中，在多GPU系统和不同模型类型中实现了最高26.3%的能效提升，并显著降低功耗约束下的QoS违规率。结果表明，将功耗控制直接整合到LLM推理运行时具有重要意义，有助于构建更节能、更灵活的AI系统。

item.title: 人工智能赋能的微波光子学综述
item.content:encoded:
AI推动微波光子学发展，提升信号处理效率与系统性能。



摘要： 本文综述了人工智能（AI）在微波光子学（MWP）中的应用与进展。MWP作为融合微波与光子技术的交叉领域，能够突破传统电子系统的带宽限制，实现高频信号的高效生成、传输和处理。近年来，AI的快速发展正在深刻改变MWP的各个层面，包括信号处理、系统设计与维护等。文章系统总结了AI在MWP中的最新成果，并为学术界和公众提供了深入见解。

item.title: 基于强化学习的交叉路口布局与信号控制优化方法
item.content:encoded:
DeCoR框架提升城市行人过街效率并优化交通信号。



摘要： 本文提出DeCoR，一个两阶段的强化学习框架，用于优化城市交叉路口布局和信号控制。该方法通过流观测数据，联合优化行人路径和交通信号时序，以减少行人和车辆的等待时间。实验在真实城市路段进行，结果显示DeCoR使行人到达最近路口的时间减少23%，同时减少交叉路口数量。在信号控制方面，行人和车辆等待时间分别降低79%和65%。此外，该控制策略具有良好的泛化能力，适应不同需求并能应对布局变化。

item.title: 基于并行变分蒙特卡洛的深度状态空间模型训练方法
item.content:encoded:
提出PVMC方法提升DSSM训练效率与效果。



摘要： 深度状态空间模型（DSSM）在统计建模中广泛应用，但大规模训练仍具挑战。现有方法主要分为两类：基于变分下界的自编码训练和基于经典序贯蒙特卡洛算法（SMC）的反向传播训练。然而，这些方法因前向传递的序列性，在现代硬件上扩展性差。本文提出一种新的训练方法——并行变分蒙特卡洛（PVMC），结合两种范式，可高效训练DSSM用于判别和生成任务。实验表明，该方法在基准测试中表现优异，且训练速度比现有最优SMC方法快10倍。

item.title: 基于泄漏感知的释放型共形分诊审计方法
item.content:encoded:
研究提出一种新的共形分诊审计方法，以确保患者安全。



摘要： 本文提出了一种泄漏感知的释放型共形分诊审计方法，用于评估在流行率变化情况下，分诊系统是否将真正发生目标事件的患者错误释放。该方法将目标对象分为三个不重叠的角色：流行率校正、共形校准和保留释放安全性评估。通过这种分离，审计可以直接评估释放情况，包括有多少事件阳性患者未被审查、校准所需的事件标签是否足够以及安全与审查之间的权衡变化。在回顾性NSCLC试点中的应用表明，较低的审查率可能具有误导性，因为经过流行率校正后，合并的共形分支释放了更多患者，其中一些是事件阳性。审计中的类别分支起到了稀缺性诊断的作用，表明试点中事件标签不足，无法保证低审查率的安全性。

item.title: 知识驱动的视觉问答基准WikiVQABench
item.content:encoded:
提出WikiVQABench，用于评估知识感知的视觉语言模型。



摘要： 本文介绍了WikiVQABench，一个基于外部知识的视觉问答基准。该基准通过结合维基百科图片、文章摘要和Wikidata结构化知识构建，利用大语言模型生成多选题，并由人工审核确保事实准确性和视觉文本一致性。该数据集包含大量维基百科图片和设计用于测试知识密集型推理能力的多选题。对15个视觉语言模型的评估显示性能范围广泛（24.7%-75.6%），证明了该基准的有效性。数据集和代码已公开。

item.title: Mem-π：一种基于生成的自适应记忆框架
item.content:encoded:
Mem-π通过生成上下文相关指导提升LLM代理性能。



摘要： 本文提出Mem-π，一种用于大型语言模型（LLM）代理的自适应记忆框架。与传统依赖外部记忆库检索的方法不同，Mem-π利用专用的语言或视觉-语言模型，在需要时生成特定上下文的指导。该模型根据当前代理上下文决定是否生成指导以及生成何种指导，并通过解耦的强化学习目标进行训练，使其在无需帮助时可以避免生成。实验表明，Mem-π在多种代理基准任务中表现优异，特别是在网页导航任务中提升了30%以上。

item.title: EvoStruct解决抗体CDR设计中的词汇崩溃问题
item.content:encoded:
EvoStruct提升抗体CDR设计的氨基酸恢复率与多样性。



摘要： 本文提出EvoStruct，通过将冻结的蛋白质语言模型与E(3)-等变图神经网络结合，解决抗体互补决定区（CDR）设计中词汇崩溃的问题。传统GNN方法在预测氨基酸时存在偏差，忽视功能重要残基。EvoStruct利用交叉注意力适配器融合结构信息与语言模型，通过逐步解冻和R-Drop正则化提高性能。实验表明，EvoStruct在CHIMERA-Bench数据集上实现了最高的氨基酸恢复率、最低的困惑度，并显著提升了序列恢复率和结合对相关性。

item.title: 基于动态集成的细粒度水果分类方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出FruitEnsemble框架提升水果分类准确率。



摘要： 本文针对农业计算机视觉中的细粒度水果分类问题，提出了一个两阶段的动态推理框架FruitEnsemble。该框架通过构建包含306类水果、116,233个样本的全面数据集，并采用验证校准的加权集成方法生成稳健的Top-3候选池。对于困难样本，引入专家仲裁机制，当集成置信度低于0.6时，触发多模态大语言模型进行严格的视觉验证。此外，优化了训练流程，采用硬样本感知联合损失函数。实验表明，FruitEnsemble在分类准确率上达到70.49%，优于现有先进模型，为实际农业视觉分拣和质量检测提供了高效的解决方案。

item.title: 在线凸优化中约束条件下的算法改进
item.content:encoded:
改进在线凸优化算法，降低约束违反度并提升性能。



摘要： 本文研究了对抗性约束下的受限在线凸优化问题。在每一轮中，学习者需在观察损失函数和约束函数前选择动作，目标是实现对满足所有约束的最佳点的小静态遗憾，并控制累积约束违反度（CCV）。对于强凸损失，现有算法可实现O(log T)的遗憾和O(√T log T)的CCV；对于凸损失，则为O(√T)的遗憾和O(√T log T)的CCV。本文提出一种基于投影的简单算法，对于强凸损失实现了O(log T)的遗憾和O(log T)的CCV，显著降低CCV。对于凸损失，该算法将CCV改进为O(√T)，同时保持最优的O(√T)遗憾。关键在于利用了自收缩曲线的几何结果，可能具有独立研究价值。

item.title: 生成模型在PDE诱导概率测度中的理论分析
item.content:encoded:
研究生成模型在偏微分方程概率测度中的理论性质与泛化能力。



摘要： 本文构建了一个理论框架，用于分析运输映射的正则性和一阶Wasserstein引导生成模型在偏微分方程（PDE）诱导概率测度中的泛化性能。研究对象包括线性椭圆和抛物方程在有界域上的归一化目标密度，以及环面上的扩散和Fokker-Planck方程。在标准结构假设下，证明了这些目标测度满足加倍条件，并结合最优传输的正则性理论，得出从均匀源测度到目标测度的最优传输映射是Hölder连续的。这一正则性为通过单次前向映射学习PDE诱导分布的生成模型提供了近似理论依据。以DeepParticle为例，推导了超出风险界限，刻画了学习映射与总体最优映射之间的差异，并在目标偏移下建立了鲁棒性估计，实验结果支持了理论分析的速率。

item.title: Stochastic MeanFlow Policies在离线策略强化学习中的应用
item.content:encoded:
提出SMFP方法提升离线策略RL的探索与稳定性。



摘要： 本文研究了在线离线策略强化学习中的两个关键选择：策略类和更新规则。传统高斯策略虽计算快但难以处理多模态动作分布，而生成策略虽更灵活但常需迭代采样或缺乏可计算熵估计。作者提出Stochastic MeanFlow Policies（SMFP），通过均值流变换将高斯噪声映射为动作，实现可计算熵并支持稳定探索。该方法结合了熵正则化与镜像下降约束，在七个MuJoCo基准测试中优于传统方法，同时保持单步推理效率。

item.title: Manga109数据集改进与标注问题分析
item.content:encoded:
改进Manga109数据集以提升OCR和漫画理解任务的准确性。



摘要： 本文针对现有Manga109数据集中存在的标注问题进行了深入分析，识别出五类主要问题，包括转录错误、缺失文本区域、对话与拟声词重叠以及语音气泡分割不足。为解决这些问题，作者结合OCR技术检测与人工修正，构建了Manga109-v2026版本，修订了约29,000条对话标注。该改进使数据集更符合现代OCR和多模态漫画理解系统的需求，同时保留了漫画特有的表达结构。

item.title: JobArabi：大规模阿拉伯语招聘公告语料库的构建与分析
item.content:encoded:
构建了涵盖两年的阿拉伯语招聘语料库，揭示了招聘语言的社会语言学特征。



摘要： 本文介绍了JobArabi，一个从2024年1月至2025年10月期间收集的阿拉伯语招聘公告大型语料库。该数据集包含20,528条来自X平台的公开帖子，覆盖阿拉伯语在线社区的就业讨论。语料库通过基于语言学的查询框架构建，涵盖21个阿拉伯语关键词族，反映了招聘语言中的性别、复数、正式和方言表达。数据包括机构、商业和个人账号的帖子，并提供时间戳、互动指标和地理位置等元数据，支持就业话语的时空分析。定量分析揭示了招聘语言中的社会语言学模式，如性别化招聘语言的持续存在、职业需求的区域差异以及招聘信息的情感框架。研究结果表明阿拉伯社交媒体是研究劳动力市场沟通和语言变化的重要资源。JobArabi语料库及文档和采集脚本将用于支持阿拉伯语NLP、计算社会科学和数字劳动研究。

item.title: CRAFT：解决联邦学习中客户端更新冲突的聚合框架
item.content:encoded:
CRAFT通过几何校正方法解决联邦学习中的更新冲突问题。



摘要： 在联邦学习中，异构数据分布下客户端更新的聚合是一个核心挑战。传统平均方法可能导致全局模型优化但损害部分客户端性能。本文提出CRAFT（Conflict-Resolved Aggregation for Federated Training），将全局更新视为几何校正问题，通过约束优化找到与参考方向最接近且无冲突的更新方向，避免迭代求解的计算开销。同时，采用分层适应策略处理不同粒度的特征冲突。理论分析表明，CRAFT能促进共同下降结构并减少冲突。实验结果表明，CRAFT在提升全局模型精度的同时，有效降低了客户端间的性能差异。

item.title: Stream3D：一种无需训练的流式3D生成方法
item.content:encoded:
Stream3D解决单视角3D生成的时序不一致问题。



摘要： 本文提出Stream3D，一种无需训练的流式3D生成机制，能够将冻结的视图条件3D生成器转换为流式生成器。通过维护一个紧凑的证据记忆，Stream3D选择性地缓存最具信息量的历史帧，并动态更新以保持固定数量的帧，从而避免内存随序列长度线性增长。该方法在真实和合成数据集上均优于现有基线，在光度和几何指标上表现优异，且无需重新训练或修改架构。

item.title: 基于时间融合变压器的动态代理模型用于海洋系统模拟
item.content:encoded:
动态代理模型实现海洋系统高效预测与不确定性分析。



摘要： 本文提出一种动态信息引导的时间融合变压器（TFT）作为计算密集型地球系统模拟的数据驱动代理模型。该模型针对全球海洋输运的多变量时间序列进行训练，能够预测数千个时间步内的关键事件。数据包含最多21个非平稳时间序列以及描述自由参数和初始条件的静态协变量。通过调整架构和目标函数，该代理模型能够高精度预测大西洋和太平洋系统的崩溃时间，并捕捉集成预测中的随机不确定性。该模型在数值模拟器上实现了465倍的计算加速，同时保持对参数和初始条件的可微性。

item.title: 提升大语言模型安全性的上下文不变对齐方法
item.content:encoded:
通过锚定不变正则化提升模型在不同语境下的安全性表现。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为锚定不变正则化（AIR）的方法，用于增强大语言模型在面对不同表述方式时的安全行为一致性。传统方法在对齐过程中难以确保行为不依赖于表面形式，而AIR通过将可验证提示作为锚点，仅对开放性任务进行正则化，从而提高模型在不同情境下的鲁棒性。实验表明，该方法在安全、道德推理和数学任务中显著提升了模型的上下文不变性和准确性。

item.title: 基于JEPA的未来动作预测方法JFAA在EK-100挑战中取得最佳成绩
item.content:encoded:
JFAA在EK-100动作预测任务中表现优异，获第一名。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于JEPA的未来动作预测方法JFAA，用于EPIC-KITCHENS-100（EK-100）动作预测任务。该方法借鉴了V-JEPA 2.1的表征学习和未来预测能力，利用冻结编码器和预测器提取观察到的上下文特征和近未来潜在标记。随后，训练一个轻量级的注意力探测器，通过独立的任务查询预测动词、名词和动作的逻辑值。为进一步提高鲁棒性，构建了一个场感知集成模型，使每个输出字段能够从最可靠的候选中受益。实验结果表明，JFAA在官方挑战服务器上取得了第一名的成绩。代码将公开在GitHub上。

item.title: 6G超密集网络中基于联邦强化学习的分布式资源管理研究
item.content:encoded:
FedCritic提升6G网络SINR与公平性，降低协调开销。



摘要： 本文研究6G超密集网络中多小区正交频分多址接入（OFDMA）的分布式下行资源管理问题，重点解决干扰耦合和长期用户服务质量（QoS）约束下的子载波调度与功率分配。通过引入虚拟队列缺陷权重以满足长期QoS要求，提出FedCritic框架，该框架采用轻量级图通信参数平均实现去中心化批评者学习，无需中央协调器即可保持策略本地化和稳定价值估计。仿真结果表明，FedCritic在高干扰场景下提升了平均信噪比与边缘速率，提高了网络整体吞吐量和公平性，相比非协调和集中训练-分散执行方法具有更稳定的训练性能和更低的协调开销。

item.title: AutoRPA：提升GUI任务执行效率的自动化框架
item.content:encoded:
AutoRPA通过自动提炼ReAct代理逻辑，显著提升GUI任务执行效率。



摘要： 本文提出AutoRPA，一个将ReAct风格代理的决策逻辑自动转化为稳健RPA功能的框架。其核心创新包括：（1）翻译-构建管道，将硬编码的ReAct操作转换为软编码流程，并通过多轨迹检索增强生成合成RPA函数；（2）在代码验证中采用混合修复策略，结合RPA执行与ReAct回退进行迭代优化。实验表明，AutoRPA生成的RPA函数在多个GUI环境中成功解决相似任务，同时减少82%至96%的token使用量，显著提高运行效率和可重用性。

item.title: 人机协作中目标塑造的贡献归因研究
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示AI在目标塑造中的实际贡献及用户认知偏差。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为CoTrace的目标级归因框架，用于分析人机协作中目标形成过程中的直接和间接贡献。通过分析638个真实协作日志，发现AI虽然仅占目标塑造贡献的11-26%，但在引入具体要求方面作用显著，并产生多种间接影响。研究还表明交互设计对AI目标塑造行为有显著影响，用户在接触目标级分析后，其对自身贡献的认知发生了明显变化，显示出用户对AI辅助工作的系统性误判。

item.title: 量子强化学习在过程合成中的应用研究
item.content:encoded:
量子强化学习提升过程合成问题的解决效率与可扩展性。



摘要： 本文提出将量子强化学习（QRL）应用于过程合成问题，构建了一个通用框架，将过程合成形式化为马尔可夫决策过程，并引入量子增强的强化学习算法以提高可扩展性。针对早期量子RL在过程合成中因量子比特需求受限的问题，本文通过状态编码算法解耦了量子比特需求与问题规模的关系。实验对比了经典RL和量子RL在不同单元数量的流程图合成问题上的性能，结果显示量子方法在小规模设计空间中能识别最优流程，中等规模下表现出良好的每回合性能和参数效率。该研究为过程系统工程中的量子计算应用奠定了基础，并建立了比较经典与量子算法的基准。

item.title: 无需训练的零样本组合图像检索方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无需训练的零样本组合图像检索方法，提升多模态检索效果。



摘要： 本文研究了无需训练的零样本组合图像检索（CIR）方法，针对现有基于大语言模型（LLM）的方法在生成目标描述时引入额外特征以及检索阶段点对点对齐不足的问题，提出了一种基于语义过渡与传输的协同框架。该方法通过嵌入空间中的过渡向量优化由LLM生成的描述，并结合用户指令过滤噪声，使描述更聚焦于核心修改意图。同时，将描述与图像建模为离散分布，将检索任务转化为集合到集合的对齐任务，并设计双向传输距离以实现细粒度跨模态对齐，从而提升检索效果。实验表明该方法在多种CIR任务中具有良好的泛化性和有效性。

item.title: AI代理在Python代码重构中的质量与风险分析
item.content:encoded:
AI代理重构代码提升部分质量但引入新问题。



摘要： 本文通过分析AIDev数据集中的Python重构PR，评估AI代理生成的代码更改对代码质量、可维护性和安全性的影响。研究发现，AI代理的提交在22.5%的改动中提升了质量属性，其中可用性提升最明显。然而，24.17%的文件引入了新的Pylint问题，4.7%引入了新的Bandit安全问题。研究还总结出24种常见的重构操作，并分析其对代码质量的影响。尽管存在局限，AI代理的PR被高比例合并，表明其在代码开发中的潜力和需要加强的质量控制。

item.title: 基于混合表示的3D感知预训练框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种结合隐式与显式表示的3D预训练框架，提升机器人任务性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种新的3D感知预训练框架，通过引入结构化潜在点来融合隐式神经场和显式几何表示的优势。该框架在点云自动编码器的潜在空间中嵌入一个点级潜在变分自编码器，联合正则化点特征与坐标以接近高斯先验，从而保留粗略结构信息并增强语义表达。同时，设计了一个轻量级3DGS渲染管道，提升效率并保留前端潜在模块的表征能力。实验表明，该方法在RLBench、ManiSkill2及真实机器人平台上均取得显著性能提升。

item.title: 基于N步前向轨迹的强化学习方法提升大语言模型推理能力
item.content:encoded:
提出NFPO算法提升RLVR性能，增强大语言模型推理能力。



摘要： 本文研究了基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）在提升大语言模型推理能力中的应用。针对传统PPO代理目标的局部性问题，作者引入了N步前向轨迹，通过累积下一个N-1个token的似然比来增强PPO代理目标。基于此，提出了N步前向轨迹策略优化（NFPO）算法，该算法将N步前向轨迹整合到掩码策略梯度框架中，为PPO代理目标与精确策略梯度目标之间提供连续桥梁，实现偏差-方差权衡的合理控制。理论分析表明，适当选择N值可获得比标准PPO更紧的策略改进界。实验结果表明，NFPO在多个推理基准测试中均表现出色，验证了其有效性。

item.title: Interpretable Discriminative Text Representations via Agreement and Label Disentanglement
item.content:encoded: Interpretable text representations should expose coordinates that are not only predictive, but also meaningful enough for independent auditors to apply. Existing discriminative representations often use anonymous embedding directions, while concept-bottleneck and LLM-assisted methods attach natural-language names to features without ensuring that those definitions are reproducible or distinct from the target label. We propose an operational criterion for interpretable discriminative text representations: each coordinate should satisfy conceptual clarity, measured by chance-adjusted agreement between independent annotators applying the feature definition, and label disentanglement, meaning the feature should not merely paraphrase the prediction target. We instantiate this criterion in LLM-assisted Feature Discovery (LFD), an iterative method that proposes lexical and semantic features from contrastive outcome-opposed text pairs, screens candidates using cross-LLM Cohen's $κ$, and selects features by residual held-out predictive gain. A stylized analysis connects the $κ$ screen to a per-feature annotation-noise bound, formalizing agreement as a reliability check. Across ten text-classification tasks spanning seven corpora, LFD matches the predictive performance of a strong text bottleneck baseline while producing substantially clearer and less label-entangled features. Human audits with 232 raters show that LFD features achieve higher human--human and human--LLM agreement than baseline concepts, and raters consistently judge them as less label-leaking. These results suggest that agreement-tested, label-disentangled coordinates provide a practical auditability standard for interpretable text classification.

item.title: 基于庞特里亚金原理的直接策略优化方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出PG-DPO方法解决非指数折扣下的强化学习问题。



摘要： 本文针对传统基于贝尔曼方程的强化学习方法在非指数折扣场景下失效的问题，指出其结构上的脆弱性。作者提出一种新的变分框架——庞特里亚金引导的直接策略优化（PG-DPO），该方法摒弃了递归机制，结合庞特里亚金最大原理与蒙特卡洛模拟，通过伴随-蒙特卡洛投影实现逐点哈密顿最大化。实验表明，在多维双曲和生存折扣基准测试中，PG-DPO在准确性和稳定性上优于传统方程求解器和基于评判器的基线方法。

查看原文

item.title: CHOIR: Contact-aware 4D Hand-Object Interaction Reconstruction
item.content:encoded: We ask whether everyday open-world monocular videos can be turned into reusable 4D interaction primitives: articulated hand motion, object shape with 6D pose over time, and the when/where of contact. Such a capability would enable scalable mining of real interactions and, beyond reconstruction, support scene-aware synthesis and planning. However, reconstructing hand-object interaction (HOI) from challenging monocular videos remains difficult: methods often assume known objects or curated scenes, and separately estimated hands and objects easily become misaligned under clutter, occlusion, and unseen object geometries. Targeting this setting, we present CHOIR, a Contact-aware HOI Reconstruction framework for a monocular camera, using contact as an explicit coupling signal between hands and objects. CHOIR first initializes a coarse, contact-agnostic 4D HOI sequence from open-world visual priors. It then introduces a generative HOI spatial rectification module to predict ray-depth corrections and rectify hand-object relative placement, then derive initial per-frame contact correspondences on the rectified geometry. Last, a contact-aware joint optimization with dynamically updated contact constraints enforces geometric, temporal, and contact consistency. Experiments on controlled and challenging videos show that CHOIR improves object reconstruction, physical plausibility, and temporal consistency over state-of-the-art methods.

item.title: Mahjax: A GPU-Accelerated Mahjong Simulator for Reinforcement Learning in JAX
item.content:encoded: Riichi Mahjong is a multi-player, imperfect-information game characterized by stochasticity and high-dimensional state spaces. These attributes present a unique combination of challenges that mirror complex real-world decision-making problems in reinforcement learning. While prior research has heavily relied on supervised learning from human play logs to pre-train the policy, algorithms capable of learning \textit{tabula rasa} (from scratch) offer greater potential for general applicability, as evidenced by the AlphaZero lineage. To facilitate such research, we introduce \textbf{Mahjax}, a fully vectorized Riichi Mahjong environment implemented in JAX to enable large-scale rollout parallelization on Graphics Processing Units (GPUs). We also provide a high-quality visualization tool to streamline debugging and interaction with trained agents. Experimental results demonstrate that Mahjax achieves throughputs of up to \textbf{2 million} and \textbf{1 million steps per second} on eight NVIDIA A100 GPUs under the no-red and red rules, respectively. Furthermore, we validate the environment's utility for reinforcement learning by showing that agents can be trained effectively to improve their rank against baseline policies.

item.title: Latent Dynamics for Full Body Avatar Animation
item.content:encoded: Pose-driven full-body avatars built on neural rendering produce high-quality novel views of a captured subject. Yet loose clothing and other dynamic elements deform in ways pose alone cannot explain: the same pose can correspond to many different states, because their motion depends on history, inertia, and contact. Explicit simulation and layered-garment methods can model such dynamics, but they require either a dedicated garment template, which raw multi-view capture does not naturally provide, or a test-time physics simulator with non-trivial runtime cost. A parallel line of work learns data-driven clothing avatars that avoid explicit garment layers. These methods add an auxiliary latent for variation beyond pose; at inference, they fix it, regress it from pose, or retrieve it from training data, without explicitly modeling how the latent evolves with its own dynamics. Additionally, even in everyday motion with loose clothing, existing architectures often struggle to capture fine-grained detail, producing blurry renderings and temporal artifacts. We augment a pose-conditioned 3D Gaussian avatar with a transformer-based decoder and a dynamics residual latent that captures temporal appearance and geometry variation beyond the driving signals. At inference, a learned latent dynamics model evolves the residual latent from a short pose history and the previous latent state. The model decomposes each update into driving, restoring, and dissipative forces, producing temporally coherent, history-dependent rollouts with negligible added cost. Different initial conditions yield diverse yet plausible motion trajectories, and the force decomposition exposes controls such as stiffness. Across nine captured sequences of everyday motion with diverse loose garments, quantitative metrics and a perceptual user study show improved animation quality over recent data-driven baselines.

item.title: Quantifying Hyperparameter Transfer and the Importance of Embedding Layer Learning Rate
item.content:encoded: Hyperparameter transfer allows extrapolating optimal optimization hyperparameters from small to large scales, making it critical for training large language models (LLMs). This is done either by fitting a scaling law to the hyperparameters or by a judicious choice of parameterization, such as Maximal Update ($μ$P), that renders optimal hyperparameters approximately scale invariant. In this paper, we first develop a framework to quantify hyperparameter transfer through three metrics: (1) the quality of the scaling law fit, (2) the robustness to extrapolation errors, and (3) the asymptotic loss penalty due to choice of parameterization. Next, we investigate through a comprehensive series of ablations why $μ$P appears to offer high-quality learning rate transfer relative to standard parameterization (SP), as existing theory is inadequate. We find that the overwhelming benefit of $μ$P relative to SP when training with AdamW arises simply from maximizing the learning rate of the embedding layer. In SP, the embedding layer learning rate acts as a bottleneck that induces training instabilities; increasing it by a factor of width to match $μ$P dramatically smooths out training while improving hyperparameter transfer. We also find that weight decay improves the scaling law fits, while, in the fixed token-per-parameter setting, it hurts the robustness of the extrapolation.

item.title: A Typed Tensor Language for Federated Learning
item.content:encoded: Federated learning and analytics are often described as collections of separate protocols, even when they share the same mathematical form: client-local tensor computation, mergeable aggregation into shared state, and shared-only post-processing. We introduce a typed tensor language that formalizes this structure. The language distinguishes federated tensors, whose records are partitioned across clients along a tracked record axis, from shared tensors, which are available globally. Its semantics are defined by comparison with a virtual global tensor, used only as a reference object. The main result is a shared-state factorization theory. We show that typed one-round programs factor through fixed-dimensional shared state whose size is independent of the number of clients and records, computed from client-local tensor expressions and merged across clients. We also prove a converse representability result; factorizations whose encoders and decoders are expressible in the language are realized by typed one-round programs, and the correspondence extends to iterative programs whose cross-round state is shared. This gives a formal account of the computations in the language that can be expressed as encode, merge, and decode procedures. We then develop a differentiable fragment for learning. If a per-record loss and its per-record gradient are represented by client-local tensor expressions, the global gradient is represented by record-axis summation of the federated gradient tensor. This yields typed iterative programs for server-side gradient descent and shared-linear-algebra second-order updates. The framework characterizes a broad class of federated learning computations whose communication passes through fixed-dimensional shared state.

item.title: A Sharper Picture of Generalization in Transformers
item.content:encoded: We study transformers' generalization behavior on boolean domains from the perspective of the Fourier Spectra of their target functions. In contrast to prior work (Edelman et al., 2022; Trauger and Tewari, 2024), which derived generalization bounds from Rademacher complexity, we investigate the feasibility of obtaining generalization bounds via PAC-Bayes theory. We show that sparse spectra concentrated on low-degree components enable low-sharpness constructions with good generalization properties. Our idea is to show the existence of flat minima implementing any boolean function of sparsity no greater than the context length, and then apply a PAC-Bayes bound to an idealized low-sharpness learner, resulting in a non-vacuous generalization bound. We evaluate predictions empirically and conduct a mechanistic interpretability study to support the realism of our theoretical construction in real transformers.

item.title: Rethinking Cross-Layer Information Routing in Diffusion Transformers
item.content:encoded: Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) have become a de facto backbone of modern visual generation, and nearly every major axis of their design -- tokenization, attention, conditioning, objectives, and latent autoencoders -- has been extensively revisited. The residual stream that governs how information accumulates across layers, however, has been directly inherited from the original Transformer. In this paper, we present a systematic empirical analysis of cross-layer information flow in DiTs, jointly along depth and denoising timestep, and identify three concrete symptoms of traditional residual addition, namely monotonic forward magnitude inflation, sharp backward gradient decay, and pronounced block-wise redundancy. Motivated by this diagnosis, we propose Diffusion-Adaptive Routing (\textsc{DAR}), a drop-in residual replacement that performs \emph{learnable, timestep-adaptive, and non-incremental} aggregation over the history of sublayer outputs. Moreover, the proposed \textsc{DAR} is compatible with many modern Transformer enhancement methods, such as REPA. On ImageNet $256\times256$, \textsc{DAR} improves SiT-XL/2 by $2.11$ FID ($7.56$ vs.\ $9.67$) and matches the baseline's converged quality with $8.75\times$ fewer training iterations. Stacked on top of REPA, it yields a $2\times$ training acceleration in the early stage, suggesting cross-layer information routing as an underexplored design axis in diffusion modeling, one that operates orthogonally to existing representation-alignment objectives. Beyond pretraining, \textsc{DAR} can also be applied during the fine-tuning stage of large-scale T2I models and preserves high-frequency details during Distribution Matching Distillation.

item.title: Is Fixing Schema Graphs Necessary? Full-Resolution Graph Structure Learning for Relational Deep Learning
item.content:encoded: Relational prediction tasks are fundamental in many real-world applications, where data are naturally stored in relational databases (RDBs). Relational Deep Learning (RDL) addresses this problem by modeling RDBs as graphs and applying graph neural networks (GNNs) for end-to-end learning. However, the full-resolution property is commonly adopted as a design principle in graph construction for RDBs to preserve relational semantics, which leads most existing methods to rely on fixed graph structures. In this paper, we propose FROG, a Full-Resolution and Optimizable Graph Structure Learning} framework for RDL that formulates relational structure learning as a learnable table role modeling problem, allowing tables to contribute as nodes and edges in message passing. We further design role-driven message passing mechanisms to capture relational semantics, enabling joint optimization of graph structure and GNN representations. To ensure semantic consistency, we introduce functional dependency constraints that regularize representations across table and entity levels. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms existing approaches and reveal how table roles impact downstream tasks, offering new insights into graph construction for RDL

item.title: RoadTones: Tone Controllable Text Generation from Road Event Videos
item.content:encoded: Existing video-language models can generate factual descriptions of road events but lack control over how these events are expressed: their tone, urgency, or style. This limits deployment in communication-critical settings where the effectiveness of a message depends on both content and presentation, not just factual accuracy. To mitigate this, we introduce a comprehensive dataset-model-evaluation suite for tone-controllable road video captioning. Our human-validated data generation pipeline expands road-video corpora with diverse tonal annotations and multi-tone captions, yielding the RoadTones-51K dataset. We propose RoadTones-VL-CoT, a controllable video-to-text model that also generates tone-conditioned Chain-of-Thought intermediate drafts for interpretability. We also introduce RoadTones-Eval, a new evaluation suite that jointly measures factual consistency and tone adherence. In addition, we conducted a user study whose results validate caption quality, tone control, and factual consistency. Together, these contributions lay the foundation for context-sensitive tone-controllable video captioning.

item.title: Preserve, Reveal, Expand: Faithful 4D Video Editing with Region-Aware Conditioning
item.content:encoded: Existing 4D-driven video diffusion models primarily target plausible generation, but faithful 4D editing requires preserving source-observed regions while synthesizing disoccluded or out-of-view content. We identify Evidence-Role Mismatch: reliable source-backed evidence, unreliable rendered cues, and unsupported regions are entangled in a single conditioning signal, causing preservation drift, ghosting, and unstable extrapolation. We propose PREX (Preserve, Reveal, Expand), a region-aware framework that decomposes the target spatiotemporal volume into Preserve, Reveal, and Expand roles according to observation support and scene extent. PREX builds observation-backed appearance cues with calibrated confidence and injects them into a frozen video diffusion backbone through a region-aware adapter, trained with proxy tasks without requiring paired edited videos. We further introduce PREBench, a diagnostic benchmark with curated edits, region-role masks, and human-aligned metrics that complement global video-quality and 4D-control evaluations. Experiments show that PREX reduces region-structured failures while maintaining strong visual quality and 4D edit control capability. Project Page: https://ricepastem.github.io/PREX-Open

item.title: RCGDet3D: Rethinking 4D Radar-Camera Fusion-based 3D Object Detection with Enhanced Radar Feature Encoding
item.content:encoded: 4D automotive radar is indispensable for autonomous driving due to its low cost and robustness, yet its point cloud sparsity challenges 3D object detection. Existing 4D radar-camera fusion methods focus on complex fusion strategies, trading inference speed for marginal gains. This trade-off hinders real-time deployment due to heavy computation on dense feature maps. In contrast, feature extraction from sparse radar points is less time-consuming but remains under-explored. This work uncovers that simply enhancing radar feature extraction can achieve comparable or even higher performance than elaborate fusion modules, while maintaining real-time performance. Based on this finding, we propose RCGDet3D, which centers on radar feature encoding and simplifies multi-modal fusion. Its encoder inherits from the efficient Gaussian Splatting-based Point Gaussian Encoder (PGE) in RadarGaussianDet3D with two key improvements. First, the Ray-centric PGE (R-PGE) predicts Gaussian attributes in ray-aligned coordinate systems before unifying them to Bird's-Eye View (BEV) space, significantly improving geometric consistency and reducing learning difficulty by decoupling the coordinate transformation from representation learning. Second, a Semantic Injection (SI) module incorporates visual cues from images, producing more geometrically accurate and semantically enriched radar features. Experiments on View-of-Delft (VoD) and TJ4DRadSet show that RCGDet3D outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both accuracy and speed, setting a new benchmark for real-time deployment.

item.title: SURF: Steering the Scalarization Weight to Uniformly Traverse the Pareto Front
item.content:encoded: Scalarization is widely used in multi-objective optimization owing to its simplicity and scalability. In many applications, the goal is to generate solutions that represent diverse user preferences, ideally with uniform coverage of the Pareto front (PF). However, uniformly sampling scalarization weights usually induces non-uniform coverage of the PF. We explain this mismatch through a geometric analysis of the scalarization path. As the scalarization weight varies, the corresponding solutions trace the PF with a generally non-uniform traversal speed. This speed induces an arc-length cumulative distribution function (CDF); inverting this CDF map yields a principled rule for selecting weights that produce uniform PF coverage. Building on this insight, we propose SURF (Sampling Uniformly along the PaReto Front). For structured problems, including bi-objective bandits, we derive closed-form expressions for this CDF map and the resulting PF-aware weight sampling rule. For general problems, SURF alternates between CDF reconstruction and weight sampling. Theoretically, we show that under provable conditions, SURF converges linearly to an unavoidable finite-sampling floor. Empirically, experiments on bandits, multi-objective-gymnasium, and multi-objective LLM alignment demonstrate that SURF efficiently achieves more uniform PF coverage than baselines.

item.title: Do LLMs Know What Luxembourgish Borrows? Probing Lexical Neology in Low-Resource Multilingual Models
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for writing assistance in small contact languages, yet it is unclear whether they respect community norms around lexical borrowing and neology. We introduce LexNeo-Bench, a 3{,}050-instance token-level benchmark derived from LuxBorrow, a large-scale Luxembourgish news corpus, where target tokens are labelled as native or as French, German, or English borrowings. Using this benchmark, we probe three multilingual LLMs across 34 prompt settings on two tasks: borrowing type classification and a binary lexical-innovation proxy (borrowing versus native). Without external context, models perform only slightly above chance on borrowing classification, so we construct a linguistic knowledge graph that encodes donor language, morphological patterns, and lexical analogues, and inject instance-specific subgraphs into the prompt. Knowledge-graph prompts raise borrowing classification accuracy from 25 -- 35\% up to 71 -- 81\% and largely close the gap between small and large models, while leaving neology detection difficult and sensitive to few-shot design. Our results show that lexicon-aware prompting is highly beneficial for robust borrowing judgments in low-resource contact languages and that lexical resources can serve as structured context for LLM evaluation. This study was carried out within the ENEOLI COST Action and examines borrowing as a form of lexical innovation in multilingual Luxembourgish data.

item.title: Neural Negative Binomial Regression for Weekly Seismicity Forecasting: Per-Cell Dispersion Estimation and Tail Risk Assessment
item.content:encoded: Standard approaches to forecasting the weekly number of earthquakes on a spatial grid rely on the Poisson distribution with a single global dispersion assumption. We show that this assumption is systematically violated in seismic data from Central Asia (2010-2024), where a likelihood-ratio test with boundary correction strongly rejects the Poisson hypothesis (p < 10^{-179}). The main contribution of this work is the EarthquakeNet architecture, which provides an endogenous per-cell estimate of the overdispersion parameter alpha via a neural network (spatial embeddings + MLP), without explicit spatial covariance specification. In contrast to existing negative binomial regression approaches in seismological forecasting, which typically assume a single global alpha, the proposed per-cell formulation allows the model to identify spatial heterogeneity in seismic clustering and to construct probabilistic risk-aware alerts via quantiles of the predicted distribution. A walk-forward evaluation (2018-2023) over four systems shows an 8.6 percent reduction in mean pinball deviation (MPD) relative to a negative binomial GLM baseline. The strongest improvements are observed in the tail regime (Y >= 5), where the continuous ranked probability score (CRPS) of the proposed model is 12.5 percent lower than that of the baseline, indicating improved calibration in extreme-event forecasting.

item.title: Adaptive Signal Resuscitation: Channel-wise Post-Pruning Repair for Sparse Vision Networks
item.content:encoded: One-shot magnitude pruning can cause severe accuracy collapse in the high-sparsity regime, even when the pruning mask preserves the largest weights. We argue that this failure reflects a granularity mismatch in post-pruning repair. Under global magnitude pruning, nearly collapsed channels can coexist with channels that retain informative activation variance within the same layer. Existing layer-wise activation repair methods apply a single correction to the whole layer, and can therefore over-amplify damaged channels while trying to restore the layer-level signal. We propose Adaptive Signal Resuscitation (ASR), a training-free channel-wise repair method that matches the granularity of repair to the granularity of damage. ASR estimates a variance-matching correction for each output channel and stabilizes it with a data-driven shrinkage rule, suppressing unreliable corrections for channels with weak post-pruning signal while preserving corrections for healthier channels. Applied before BatchNorm recalibration, ASR requires only forward passes on a small calibration set and no retraining. Across three datasets, four convolutional architectures, and both unstructured and structured sparsity settings, ASR generally improves over layer-wise repair, with the clearest gains in high-sparsity regimes. On ResNet-50 at 90% sparsity, ASR recovers 55.6% top-1 accuracy on CIFAR-10, compared with 41.0% for layer-wise repair and 28.0% for BatchNorm-only recalibration. Ablations show that naive channel-wise variance matching is insufficient, and that shrinkage stabilizes post-pruning repair.

item.title: Causal Past Logic for Runtime Verification of Distributed LLM Agent Workflows
item.content:encoded: Distributed LLM agent workflows should not be monitored as if they produced a single sequential log. In an asynchronous execution, a decision can only depend on events that are causally visible to the lifeline that makes it: an event that appears earlier in some log may still be unknown locally. We extend the ZipperGen agent-workflow framework with Causal Past Logic (CPL), a small past-time temporal logic for guards in conditionals and while loops. In addition to standard past-time modalities such as previous and since, a guard can inspect the latest causally visible event of another lifeline and selected variables stored there. The formula is a source-level guard: it is evaluated online by the owner lifeline and can influence control flow at runtime. We give a vector-clock monitor with latest-value views and prove that the locally computed monitor value coincides with the denotational semantics of the guard at the current event. Thus runtime verification becomes part of the coordination language itself, rather than a post-hoc check over an execution log.

item.title: AiraXiv: An AI-Driven Open-Access Platform for Human and AI Scientists
item.content:encoded: Recent advances in artificial intelligence (AI) have accelerated the growth of both human-authored and AI-generated research outputs, placing increasing strain on traditional academic publishing systems and challenging the scalability of conference- and journal-centered paradigms amid rising submission volumes, reviewer workload, and venue size. To address these challenges, we explore an AI-era publishing paradigm in which both human and AI scientists participate as authors and readers, and papers evolve through continuous, feedback-driven iteration. We propose AiraXiv, an AI-driven open-access platform built on open preprints, AI-augmented analysis and review, and reader feedback. AiraXiv supports human scientists through an interactive UI and AI scientists through Model Context Protocol (MCP)-based interactions. We validate AiraXiv through real-world deployments, including serving as the submission platform for ICAIS 2025, demonstrating its potential as a fast, inclusive, and scalable research infrastructure for the AI era. AiraXiv is publicly available at https://airaxiv.com.

item.title: Towards UAV Detection in the Real World: A New Multispectral Dataset UAVNet-MS and a New Method
item.content:encoded: The proliferation of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) has created urgent demand for precise UAV monitoring. Existing RGB-based systems rely on spatial cues that degrade at small scales, particularly with high inter-type similarity, target-clutter ambiguity, and low contrast. Multispectral imaging (MSI) encodes material-aware spectral signatures, yet MSI-based fine-grained small-UAV detection remains underexplored due to lack of dedicated datasets. We introduce UAVNet-MS, the first multispectral dataset for fine-grained small-UAV detection, comprising 15,618 temporally synchronized RGB-MSI data cubes (1440x1080) with bounding box annotations. The dataset features challenging small objects (93.7% <= 32^2 pixels, average 18^2 pixels, ~0.02% image area) under low contrast. We propose MFDNet, a dual-stream baseline addressing array-induced parallax and spatial-spectral fusion. Extensive evaluation under RGB-only, MSI-only, and RGB+MSI protocols against 20 detectors shows MFDNet achieves +6.2% AP50 improvement over best RGB-only methods, demonstrating spectral cues provide complementary material evidence beyond spatial cues. This work provides foundational dataset, strong baseline, and benchmark for multispectral UAV monitoring research.

item.title: Modular Multimodal Classification Without Fine-Tuning: A Simple Compositional Approach
item.content:encoded: We introduce CoMET, \textit{\textbf{C}omposing \textbf{M}odality \textbf{E}ncoders with \textbf{T}abular foundation models}, a simple yet highly competitive method for multimodal classification: pass each modality through a frozen pre-trained backbone, compress the resulting embeddings with PCA, and concatenate as input into a Tabular Foundation Model (TFM) for prediction. We show that PCA alone suffices to act as an adaptor yielding strong, robust performance across modalities. When the \texttt{CLS} tokens of the foundation model align poorly with downstream tasks, we propose \textbf{PALPooling}, a lightweight adaptive token pooler that consistently improves representation quality. By composing strong frozen representation learning backbones with TFMs, our approach achieves state-of-the-art results across diverse multimodal benchmarks without any training. On hierarchical tasks with large fine-grained class spaces, our approach enables fast and scalable classification, handling datasets with over 500,000 samples and 2,000 classes without any fine-tuning. Overall, our results show that the composition of foundation models is a simple, yet powerful, out-of-the-box solution for multimodal learning, challenging the necessity of complex, end-to-end training pipelines for new problems.

item.title: OSGNet with MLLM Reranking @ Ego4D Episodic Memory Challenge 2026
item.content:encoded: In this report, we present our champion solutions for the Natural Language Queries and GoalStep tracks of the Ego4D Episodic Memory Challenge at CVPR 2026. Both tracks require accurately localizing temporal segments from long untrimmed egocentric videos. To address these tasks, we propose a reranking-based framework that effectively leverages the strong video-language reasoning capability of multimodal large language model (MLLM) while preserving the efficiency and candidate recall of conventional localization pipelines. Specifically, we first obtain a set of candidate segments from existing localization model OSGNet, and then employ MLLM to select the segment that best matches the given query, thereby refining the final prediction. Ultimately, our method achieved first place in both the Natural Language Queries and GoalStep tracks. Our code can be found at https://github.com/iLearn-Lab/CVPR25-OSGNet.

item.title: CADENet：一种无需训练的异步双流增强网络用于恶劣天气下的目标检测
item.content:encoded:
CADENet在恶劣天气下实现高效目标检测，提升感知安全性。



摘要： 本文提出CADENet，一种无需训练的三线程系统，用于在恶劣天气条件下提升自动驾驶车辆的目标检测性能。该系统包含三个模块：Thread S（YOLOv11n）以全帧率进行检测，无额外延迟；Thread Q通过条件自适应增强和熵引导非极大值抑制融合结果；Thread E利用CLIP进行零样本天气分类。实验表明，在DAWN数据集上，CADENet在雪天和雨天的召回率和F1值均有提升，且无需重新训练或额外硬件。由于标注完整性偏差，报告的F1值为下限，而召回率是不受标注缺口影响的关键指标。

item.title: JAXenstein：基于JAX的视觉第一人称任务基准
item.content:encoded:
JAXenstein 提供了高效的视觉第一人称任务基准，提升算法开发速度。



摘要： 文章介绍了 JAXenstein，一个基于 JAX 的开源基准，用于视觉第一人称任务的快速和可扩展实验。该基准整合了 Wolfenstein 3D 渲染引擎，解决了当前 JAX 强化学习生态系统中缺乏复杂视觉任务测试的问题。JAXenstein 相比其他视觉基准更快，并且易于扩展到更复杂的领域，有助于提升强化学习算法的开发效率。

item.title: 面向目标的下游无关攻击方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新的目标型下游无关攻击方法，提升攻击效果。



摘要： 本文提出了一种新的目标型下游无关攻击（TDAA）方法，在更严格的威胁模型下实现对预训练编码器的攻击。传统方法只需改变原始预测即可成功，而本文要求攻击同时具备目标性和下游无关性。为解决这一难题，作者引入了‘威胁图像’作为目标，并设计生成器生成特定于每张图像的对抗扰动，使编码器对DAEs和威胁图像输出相同特征。该方法克服了以往统一扰动策略在图像多样性下的失败问题，实验证明其有效性并揭示了预训练编码器的显著漏洞。

item.title: 评估人工视觉模型与人脑视觉皮层对齐的新框架
item.content:encoded:
新框架揭示模型与人脑响应维度的对齐情况。



摘要： 本文提出一种统一框架，用于评估人工视觉模型与人脑视觉皮层之间的对齐程度。该框架通过识别可重复预测的脑响应维度，评估模型和人脑之间的匹配情况。研究利用fMRI数据发现，早期到中晚期视觉皮层包含一组低维、可重复的响应维度。通过比较不同受试者之间的脑响应，识别出可一致恢复的维度，为模型评估提供更全面的人类参考标准。结果显示，即使预测准确度相似，不同模型在响应维度上的恢复情况仍可能不同，表明仅靠预测准确度无法完全反映模型与人脑的匹配程度。

item.title: 基于约束对称性的局部搜索邻域自动生成研究
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种自动生成局部搜索邻域的方法。



摘要： 本文探讨了将约束优化问题的对称性与局部搜索邻域联系起来的方法，并在IDP系统中利用这一联系从约束规范中自动生成邻域。通过六种经典优化问题的实验评估，验证了该方法的可行性。研究结果表明，这种技术能够有效提升组合优化问题的求解效率，减少人工干预的需求。

item.title: 基于MuJoCo的ARC-RL环境在仿生机器人强化学习中的应用
item.content:encoded:
ARC-RL提供四个仿生机器人环境用于强化学习研究。



摘要： 本文介绍了ARC-RL，一个包含四个基于ARC Raiders角色设计的MuJoCo连续控制环境，分别包括18自由度的Queen六足机器人、12自由度的Bastion装甲六足机器人、18自由度的Tick紧凑六足机器人和12自由度的Leaper四足机器人。所有环境共享统一的观测模板、动作规范、模拟频率和多组件奖励函数，仅通过权重和参数调整实现形态差异。奖励函数融合了速度跟踪、生存奖励、步态合规性、动作正则化、安全惩罚和姿态锚点，未使用运动捕捉数据。文章还提供了每种形态的手工中央模式发生器演示，用于在线和离线训练，并对多种强化学习算法进行了对比实验。

item.title: 提升视觉语言模型文档解析能力的布局先验方法
item.content:encoded:
通过预处理布局信息提升VLM文档解析性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种改进视觉语言模型（VLM）文档解析能力的方法，解决其在训练数据外布局上表现不佳的问题。作者指出，VLM在解析文档时存在两步瓶颈：首先需识别布局实体，再提取内容。当第一步失败时，第二步会引发遗漏或结构错误。为解决此问题，研究者在解码器外部预先处理第一步，使用轻量级RT-DETR检测器，并将结果以DocTags格式注入提示中。该方法在多个基准测试中显著提升了性能，如markdown F1从0.37提升至0.92，表格TEDS从0.01提升至0.36，同时仅增加15%的延迟和少量提示token。分析显示，解码器在生成结构时关注布局标记，在生成内容时关注图像块，表明两步瓶颈得到缓解。

item.title: Bangladesh haor湿地洪水预警系统开发与应用
item.content:encoded:
Bangladesh haor湿地洪水预警系统提升防灾能力。



摘要： 文章介绍了针对孟加拉国haor湿地突发性洪水的预警系统HaorFloodAlert。该系统基于机器学习，能预测72小时内的洪水概率，利用温度作为季节性特征提升准确性，并通过Sentinel-1 SAR数据提供36小时预警时间。系统在77个真实事件中达到89.6%的准确率，结合三层警报机制和水稻损失评估模型，有效应对当地洪水灾害。

item.title: 基于异构监督的森林地上生物量估计方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出StruMPL模型解决森林生物量估计中的数据不匹配问题。



摘要： 本文研究如何从地球观测数据中估算森林地上生物量（AGB），面临的主要挑战是空间激光雷达提供树冠结构但无生物量数据，而地面样地提供生物量但结构信息不足。文章将此问题建模为多任务密集回归，在异构、不完全监督和缺失不随机标签的条件下进行求解，并提出StruMPL模型。该模型通过共享编码器结合回归、插补和倾向性头实现空间标签校正，同时引入可学习的物理模块以满足任务间的约束。实验表明，StruMPL在两个生态区均优于现有方法，显著降低了高生物量偏差。

item.title: 基于离散自回归的MRI重建方法
item.content:encoded:
通过离散自回归模型提升MRI重建精度。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于离散自回归的MRI重建方法，旨在解决高加速下图像重建模糊的问题。传统方法在像素域中容易平均掉高频解剖结构，而该方法将重建过程转移到离散多尺度潜在空间，并将其建模为自回归预测问题。借助视觉自回归建模中的离散先验，该方法通过代码本词序列限制解空间，从而实现从极稀疏测量中获得清晰重建。此外，研究引入了基于策略的特权信息蒸馏技术，利用教师模型在训练时提供的全采样数据监督学生模型，显著提升了重建性能。实验表明，该方法在fastMRI基准上表现出色。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的粒子滤波方法在高维动态系统中的应用
item.content:encoded:
扩散模型提升粒子滤波在高维非线性系统中的性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种利用扩散模型实现最优粒子滤波的方法，解决了传统方法在高维非线性系统中可扩展性差的问题。该方法无需额外训练，能够有效处理如大气动力学等复杂系统，实验结果表明其在高维场景下表现优异。

item.title: 提升医学大视觉语言模型可信度的因果评估与MedFocus方法
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示医学LVLM解释不足，提出MedFocus提高可信度。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）在医学应用中的可信度问题，指出其无法准确基于视觉证据进行推理。通过构建因果评估框架，验证了现有解释方法的不足，并提出MedFocus方法，利用概念驱动的归因技术，精准定位解剖区域并评估其对模型输出的影响，显著提升了医学LVLM的可信度。

item.title: 医疗AI诊断公平性优化方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种公平性正则化方法，提升医疗AI在不同群体间的诊断公平性。



摘要： 本文研究了医疗AI在不同人口群体中的诊断性能差异问题，指出AUC指标可能掩盖实际的临床不公平现象。在固定推理操作点下，某些群体可能出现过度诊断或诊断不足的情况，导致临床决策中的不平等。为解决这一问题，作者提出了一种基于最差群体等几率边缘的正则化方法，通过识别并惩罚在真实阳性率和假阳性率上偏离最大的子群体，实现多维度人口属性下的公平性优化。实验表明，该方法在保持诊断性能的同时有效减少了等几率和等机会的不公平现象。

item.title: 代码整洁度对自主编码代理行为的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
代码整洁度不影响任务完成率，但影响代理效率。



摘要： 随着自主编码代理的广泛应用，现有评估主要关注任务完成率，而忽视了代码结构和风格（即“整洁度”）对代理操作的影响。本研究通过构建最小对数据集，对比分析整洁与不整洁代码对代理行为的影响。实验发现，代码整洁度不影响代理的任务通过率，但显著降低其计算开销和文件重访次数，表明传统维护性原则在AI开发中依然重要。代码整洁度成为影响代理行为的关键因素之一。

item.title: 基于块近似稀疏注意力的高效扩散语言模型方法
item.content:encoded:
提出BA-Att框架提升扩散语言模型效率与稳定性。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为BA-Att的块近似稀疏注意力框架，通过块级预下采样操作识别信息丰富的区域，避免依赖脆弱的位置先验。该方法定义了一个理想后下采样注意力图，并形式化了预下采样和后下采样方案之间的近似误差。基于此，引入轻量级范数排序模块和协方差补偿校正，以对角QK方差近似全协方差，降低计算复杂度。实验表明，该算子在注意力计算上比FlashAttention快6.95倍，在50%稀疏性下仍保持接近全注意力性能，适用于多种语言模型和视频生成任务，展现出强大的效率和泛化能力。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的可解释虚假信息检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出LONSREX提升可解释虚假信息检测效果。



摘要： 文章探讨了社交媒体上虚假信息传播的问题，指出传统虚假信息检测方法依赖小型模型且缺乏透明度。随着大语言模型（LLM）的发展，可解释性检测成为可能，但现有方法主要依赖复杂提示生成理由，存在局限性。本文提出一种微调专用LLM的流程，并发现单纯依赖标签正确性过滤数据存在两个问题：一是标签粒度粗导致理由不足，二是强模型过度验证产生冗余理由。为解决这些问题，作者引入LONSREX，通过量化验证步骤对最终预测的贡献来评估其必要性和充分性，实验表明该方法有效。

item.title: 联邦学习中差分隐私的下界分析
item.content:encoded:
研究联邦学习中的差分隐私下界，适用于多种参数估计任务。



摘要： 本文证明了在任意公共转录交互下的联邦学习协议的通用下界。该协议可以使用任意数量的自适应轮次，且每个客户端的本地样本可在多轮中重复使用。针对平方ℓ₂损失下的参数估计，建立了满足客户级样本级零集中差分隐私（zCDP）约束的联邦 van Trees 下界。主要技术工具是完整公共转录的隐私-信息收缩不等式，并通过均值估计、线性回归和非参数回归等应用进行了验证。

item.title: 语言变异对阴谋论在社交媒体上持续传播的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
语言变异延长阴谋论的传播寿命，影响其持久性。



摘要： 本研究通过分析三年来X平台上的阴谋论相关帖子，结合计算语言学分析和生存模型，发现具有更多语义变异的阴谋论声明具有更长的生命周期。心理语言属性（如代词、社会参照词、认知过程术语、风险与健康相关词汇）以及行动者、动作和目标（AAT）类别的变异也与更长的生命周期相关。定性分析识别出两种主要的变异模式：简化和同化，涉及语言和AAT结构层面。研究结果有助于理解语言变异如何促进阴谋论的在线持续传播，并为长期内容审核策略提供参考。作者认为，内容审核应关注阴谋论声明的可变性，重点在于核心声明以应对潜在变化。

item.title: FlexDraft：一种适应不同批量大小的无损推测解码框架
item.content:encoded:
FlexDraft提升LLM推理效率，适应不同批量大小。



摘要： 本文提出FlexDraft，一种无损推测解码框架，通过三种关键设计提升大批次推理效率。首先，Attention Tuning通过调整注意力投影器实现块扩散生成，保持高质量输出；其次，Bonus-guided Calibration利用轻量MLP校准草案日志，减少验证不匹配；最后，Flex Decoding根据批次大小动态切换并行或顺序解码，并基于草案置信度调整验证长度，提高吞吐量并减少冗余计算。

item.title: 基于光照先验的低光图像增强方法InterLight
item.content:encoded:
InterLight通过利用光照先验提升低光图像增强效果。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为InterLight的新框架，用于解决低光图像增强（LLIE）中的挑战。传统方法常因过增强或色彩失真而效果不佳，且假设噪声均匀或光照理想。InterLight通过引入传感器级光照响应先验，并结合场景的潜在光照状态生成自适应提示，构建了一个光照感知的增强流程。该方法利用亮度门控的内在记忆机制，选择性补偿信息损失，同时通过自监督一致性目标进行正则化，提升图像清晰度和视觉一致性。实验表明，该方法在多个基准测试中表现优异。

item.title: 基于双贝叶斯机制的最优学习率框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种双贝叶斯框架优化学习率，提升模型训练效果。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于贝叶斯统计的双贝叶斯决策机制，用于优化随机梯度下降中的学习率。该框架通过两个对抗的贝叶斯过程推导出理论上最优的学习率，从而提升神经网络训练的效率和性能。实验表明，该方法在多种分类、分割和检测任务中均表现出良好的效果，并对模型训练和性能产生了重要影响。

item.title: 大语言模型对科学写作风格的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
大语言模型改变了科学写作的风格，影响了文本可读性和多样性。



摘要： 本文通过分析2020至2024年间37,000篇ACL Anthology论文以及3,000篇人工撰写与LLM优化文本的对比数据，探讨了大语言模型对科学写作风格的影响。研究发现，随着时间推移，词汇频率和使用语境发生了显著变化，部分领域出现语义专业化，部分则趋于泛化。进一步分析显示，LLM优化后的文本更常使用复杂句式、较长词汇，但词汇多样性降低。通过20位领域专家的初步标注研究，发现LLM优化文本在可读性和吸引力方面表现更好，但专家也表达了对AI辅助写作的负面主观态度，反映出AI对阅读体验的深刻影响。

item.title: 基于区间传播的可证明公平性修复框架ProF
item.content:encoded:
ProF通过区间传播实现可证明的模型公平性修复。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为ProF的新型公平性修复框架，利用区间传播技术对模型输出进行严格验证，以确保在偏见样本周围区域的公平性。该方法将公平性约束与模型修改整合为统一的约束求解问题，并转化为可由现成求解器处理的混合整数线性规划问题。实验表明，ProF在四个基准数据集上实现了高达95.93%的泛化公平性修复效果，并支持多种敏感属性和公平定义，显著提升了模型的公平性表现。

item.title: 基于注意力机制的大型推理模型攻击方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究发现LRM的攻击成功率与注意力模式相关，提出新的RL攻击方法提升效果。



摘要： 本文研究了大型推理模型（LRMs）在面对 jailbreak 攻击时的安全性问题，发现攻击成功率（ASR）与模型的注意力模式密切相关。成功攻击通常表现为对输入提示中的有害标记关注较低，而对推理内容中的相关标记关注较高。受此启发，作者提出一种基于强化学习（RL）的新型攻击方法，通过将注意力信号引入奖励函数设计来增强攻击效果，并结合多种说服策略扩展RL动作空间，显著提升了攻击成功率。实验结果表明，该方法在多个基准测试中优于现有技术，在有效性、效率和可迁移性方面表现突出。

item.title: 人工智能在环境科学中的应用与创新
item.content:encoded:
AI提升环境预测与决策能力，解决复杂生态问题。



摘要： 本文探讨了人工智能在环境科学中的应用，重点解决洪水预测、天气预报和科学问答三个关键问题。针对沿海河流系统的洪水管理，提出基于深度学习的WaLeF和FIDLAr模型，显著提升了预测精度与效率。在天气预测方面，开发了条件扩散模型CoDiCast，实现高效且可解释的不确定性量化。为应对大语言模型在科学问答中的幻觉问题，提出Hypercube-RAG框架，兼顾准确性、效率与可解释性。研究展示了AI在环境智能领域的强大潜力。

item.title: 基于神经网络的无限维正交基优化方法
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种神经网络优化正交基的方法。



摘要： 本文研究了如何利用神经网络来表示和优化无限维正交基。传统正交基如傅里叶基或小波基是固定的，无法适应特定问题或数据集。作者提出将目标正交基视为正交群流形上的点，并通过神经网络定义微分方程生成器，从而在函数空间中参数化和优化这些基。尽管使用有限秩生成器建模无限算子看似受限，但作者证明了其通用性：即使使用秩为2的生成器，也能在适当拓扑下逼近任意目标正交基。实验展示了该框架在转换傅里叶基到主成分、线性算子特征函数或能量守恒物理模拟动态模式中的灵活性。

item.title: 基于微状态的EEG通用表示学习研究
item.content:encoded:
微状态提升EEG表示学习效果，适用于多种脑机接口任务。



摘要： 本文研究了利用EEG信号中的微状态作为通用表示方法，通过聚类连续EEG信号生成离散微状态序列，并在睡眠分期、情绪识别和运动想象分类等任务中验证其有效性。实验表明，微状态表示优于传统时域和频域特征，在不同模型和任务中均表现优异。进一步分析显示，微状态具有更高的可解释性和扩展性，为认知神经科学和临床研究提供了新方向。

item.title: 基于影响函数的NLP模型可解释性增强研究
item.content:encoded:
影响函数提升NLP模型在样本和概念层面的可解释性。



摘要： 近年来，深度学习模型的黑箱特性限制了其在医疗诊断和金融等高风险领域的应用，而可解释性至关重要。本文提出一种基于影响函数的新方法，用于增强NLP模型在样本和概念层面的可解释性。实验表明，影响函数能够有效识别对模型预测有显著影响的训练样本，通过调整这些样本的标签和权重，可以在不重新训练的情况下恢复模型性能。此外，概念层面的分析揭示了概念瓶颈模型中影响预测的关键概念，修改这些概念可明显改变模型行为，从而提供清晰的决策过程洞察。

item.title: 基于视频生成的物理世界模拟器PhyWorld
item.content:encoded:
PhyWorld提升视频生成的物理一致性与真实性。



摘要： 文章提出PhyWorld，一种用于训练物理AI系统的视频生成世界模型。通过两阶段后训练，第一阶段使用流匹配微调提升视频连续性，第二阶段通过直接偏好优化增强物理合理性。实验表明，PhyWorld在视频一致性和物理可信度方面优于现有基准，显示出其作为物理AI模拟器的潜力。

item.title: 旋转量化方法的理论比较与新型块球量化算法
item.content:encoded:
本文比较了多种旋转量化方法并提出新型块球量化算法。



摘要： 本文对EDEN、RabitQ和TurboQuant等旋转量化方法进行了统一的理论分析，指出其性能优势依赖于不同的失真准则。研究发现，EDEN和TurboQuant在均方误差（MSE）方面表现优异，而RabitQ在高概率控制上更具优势。此外，作者提出了一种基于球面几何的新型块量化算法BlockQuant，该方法通过在球面上对块进行量化，更准确地保留了旋转嵌入的几何结构。理论分析和实验结果表明，BlockQuant在重建均方误差和期望内积失真方面均优于现有方法。
